name: VibecodeApp Observable Run Monitor
description:
Implement an event-driven observable run monitor for VibecodeApp.

Product goal:
VibecodeApp should become an observable control layer around OpenCode. The operator should be able to run a task and see two parallel truth surfaces:
1. the OpenCode / agent console output,
2. the VibecodeApp monitor stream showing what VibecodeApp prepared, what prompt/context it gave to the agent, what the agent asked from VibecodeApp via MCP, what changed in the repository, where advisory guards warned, and why.

Important product decision:
Guards are advisory by default. They must not hard-block the run yet. The default behavior should be transparent warning, drift reporting, and review guidance. Strict/blocking mode may be introduced as an explicit option only, but the main default is advisory.

Architectural goal:
Do not bolt a second console onto the existing batch command. Build a small event spine first. CLI, TUI, future GUI, JSONL logs, run replay, and tests must all consume the same run events. UI is only a view over recorded truth.

Core invariants:
- Preserve current CLI behavior unless a task explicitly changes it.
- No hidden enforcement by default.
- Every important run action must be observable as a structured event.
- Every run must be replayable from files under .vibecode/runs/<session_id>/.
- Prompt/context snapshots must be tied to a concrete run, not only stored in .vibecode/current.
- Guard findings must be understandable to a human: what happened, where, why it matters, and what to do next.
- Reviews must be written as artifacts and correction tasks must apply only grounded, actionable fixes.

tasks.title: P0.1 Audit current run, guard, context, MCP, and TUI surfaces
tasks.description:
Create a focused implementation audit before changing behavior.

Inspect the current repository and produce a concise but concrete audit artifact at:
docs/audit/OBSERVABLE_RUN_MONITOR_BASELINE.md

The audit must cover:
- Current CLI entry points related to run/context/guard/check/handoff/dashboard/serve.
- How vibecode run currently creates context packs and OpenCode prompts.
- How vibecode run currently invokes OpenCode or other external platform commands.
- Where stdout/stderr/exit code are captured.
- Where run summary files are written today.
- How guard findings are represented today.
- Whether guard failures currently affect overall status or process exit behavior.
- How MCP server tools are implemented today and whether they can be instrumented.
- How existing TUI/dashboard code is structured and whether it can be reused.
- Current tests that cover run/guard/context/MCP/TUI behavior.
- Known risk areas and likely files to touch.

Do not implement the monitor in this task. This is a baseline truth record only.
Run at least:
- python -m compileall vibecode
- pytest, or the narrowest available test command if full pytest is too slow.
Record exactly what was run and whether it passed.

tasks.title: P1.1 Implement structured event spine
tasks.description:
Implement the foundational event model for observable runs.

Likely new file:
vibecode/events.py

Required concepts:
- VibecodeEvent as a serializable dataclass or equivalent typed structure.
- Stable event fields:
  - event_id
  - session_id
  - timestamp
  - type
  - level: debug/info/warning/error
  - message
  - data
- Event type constants or enum-like names for run lifecycle, context, prompt, agent process, MCP, git delta, guard, checks, handoff, and summary.
- EventSink protocol/interface with emit(event).
- InMemoryEventSink for tests.
- JsonlEventSink that appends one JSON object per line.
- ConsoleEventSink that can print compact readable messages.
- MultiEventSink that fans out events to multiple sinks.
- NullEventSink for call sites that do not care.
- Helper function to create events consistently, including timestamp/session_id.

Design rules:
- Keep this module dependency-light and boring.
- Do not import high-level run/guard/context modules from events.py.
- JSON serialization must be deterministic enough for tests.
- Event data must accept plain JSON-compatible values only.
- If non-JSON values are passed, either convert safely or fail with a clear error.

Tests:
Add focused tests for:
- event serialization roundtrip,
- JSONL append behavior,
- multi sink fan-out,
- in-memory sink capture,
- invalid/non-serializable data handling if implemented.

Run:
- python -m compileall vibecode
- pytest tests related to events, or full pytest if practical.

  - checks_report.json
  - handoff_report.json or handoff_report.md if applicable
  - agent_stdout.log
  - agent_stderr.log
- Provide safe helpers to copy/snapshot current prompt and context files into the run directory.
- Do not remove existing .vibecode/current behavior yet; run snapshots are additive.
- Provide a way for run code to create JsonlEventSink pointing to the session events.jsonl.

tasks.title: P3.1 Refactor run command around observable run controller
tasks.description:
Refactor vibecode run so it can emit structured events without breaking normal CLI use.

Goal:
Create a RunController or equivalent orchestration class/function that receives:
- repo root/config,
- task,
- platform,
- optional model/profile/permissions args if currently supported,
- event sink,
- session artifact paths.

It should orchestrate the same high-level behavior as the current run command:
- git/preflight status,
- index freshness decision,
- context pack generation,
- prompt generation,
- external agent invocation,
- post-run guard/check/handoff,
- summary writing.

Required event emissions:
- RunStarted
- GitPreflightStarted / GitPreflightCompleted / GitPreflightWarning
- IndexCheckStarted / IndexCheckCompleted
- ContextPackStarted / ContextPackWritten
- PromptWritten
- AgentStarted
- AgentFinished
- GuardStarted / GuardCompleted
- ChecksStarted / ChecksCompleted
- HandoffStarted / HandoffCompleted
- RunSummaryWritten
- RunFinished

Rules:
- Avoid a giant rewrite. Extract, then instrument.
- Existing command-line behavior should remain usable.
- If event sink is absent, use NullEventSink.
- The CLI may still print a compact summary, but important truth must come from events and run artifacts.
- Do not implement live TUI yet.
- Do not change guard advisory/blocking semantics yet unless strictly necessary; that is P5.

Tests:
- use fake/minimal platform invocation where possible,
- prove a run emits a sensible ordered set of events,
- prove summary is still written,
- prove failures produce error events,
- prove legacy CLI path still works at least through narrow tests.

Run compile and affected tests.

tasks.title: P4.1 Add streaming agent process output
tasks.description:
Change external agent execution from purely batch-style capture to streaming-capable execution.

Goal:
When OpenCode runs, VibecodeApp should be able to stream agent stdout/stderr events live while still preserving final logs and exit code.

Required behavior:
- Add a streaming process runner abstraction if none exists.
- Use subprocess.Popen or equivalent to read stdout/stderr incrementally.
- Emit AgentStdout events for stdout lines/chunks.
- Emit AgentStderr events for stderr lines/chunks.
- Write full stdout to .vibecode/runs/<session_id>/agent_stdout.log.
- Write full stderr to .vibecode/runs/<session_id>/agent_stderr.log.
- Preserve final exit code.
- Preserve existing batch behavior for non-monitor CLI if needed, but prefer one implementation path.
- Handle Windows safely.
- Avoid deadlock when both stdout and stderr produce output.
- Do not attempt full interactive PTY/ConPTY support in this task. This is streaming output MVP, not an embedded terminal.

Tests:
- fake process that prints stdout and exits 0,
- fake process that prints stderr and exits nonzero,
- mixed stdout/stderr if practical,
- verify events and log files.

Run compile and affected tests.

tasks.title: P5.1 Make guard behavior advisory by default
tasks.description:
Update run-level guard handling so guard findings are warning/reporting truth by default, not hard enforcement.

Product rule:
Guard must say what failed, where drift happened, and why it matters. But default run behavior must not hard-block solely because guard found issues.

Required behavior:
- Introduce an explicit guard mode concept if useful:
  - advisory/default
  - strict/blocking optional
- In advisory mode:
  - guard findings are preserved,
  - severity is preserved,
  - run summary says something like needs_review or completed_with_warnings when appropriate,
  - OpenCode exit code truth is not hidden,
  - guard warnings do not automatically convert the whole run into failure.
- In strict mode, if implemented:
  - preserve existing hard-fail behavior or a clearly documented blocking behavior.
- CLI help/docs must make clear that advisory is default.
- Existing tests that expect guard failure to equal run failure must be updated only if they represent old default semantics.
- Add tests proving advisory guard does not hard-block.

Be careful:
Do not water down the findings themselves. Severity can still be error. The difference is default enforcement policy, not truth.

Run compile and affected tests.

tasks.title: P6.1 Improve guard findings into human-readable drift reports
tasks.description:

Goal:

For each finding, expose or derive:
- severity,
- rule_id,
- category/domain,
- file path or affected area,
- concise title,
- what happened,
- why it matters,
- suggested fix,
- evidence/context,
- related check/test if known.

Required outputs:
- machine-readable guard_report.json in the run session folder,
- human-readable guard_report.md in the run session folder,
- GuardFindingEmitted events for individual findings or grouped findings,
- GuardCompleted event with counts by severity/category.

UI/report style:
- Group findings by severity and category.
- Keep messages concise but actionable.
- Include exact paths.
- Do not bury the operator in raw JSON unless they ask for detail.

Tests:
- report generated when findings exist,
- report generated or skipped clearly when no findings exist,
- grouped counts are correct,
- event data includes key fields.

tasks.title: P8.1 Add MCP tool-call observability
tasks.description:
Instrument VibecodeApp MCP tools so the operator can see what the agent asked VibecodeApp.

Goal:

- tool name,
- sanitized arguments,

- error if any,
- timestamp,
- optional session_id if available.

Required behavior:
- Add event/logging hooks around MCP tools such as get_file_card, find_symbol, list_high_risk if they exist.
- Emit or write:
  - McpToolCalled
  - McpToolReturned
  - McpToolFailed
- If MCP server runs out-of-process from the run controller, use a safe fallback log path such as .vibecode/logs/mcp_events.jsonl.
- If a VIBECODE_SESSION_ID environment variable or similar session correlation can be passed to the MCP server, implement it narrowly and document it.
- Do not dump huge MCP responses. Store a compact result summary:
  - number of symbols,
  - number of files,
  - risk count,
  - approximate chars,
  - path queried,
  - error message if failed.
- Preserve full tool correctness.

Tests:
- direct tool call produces log/event,
- failures are logged,
- result summary avoids large blobs,

tasks.title: P9.1 Implement TUI two-pane monitor MVP
tasks.description:
Implement the first operator-facing monitor command.

Goal:
A TUI command should run an OpenCode task and show two live panes:
- left pane: OpenCode/agent stdout and stderr stream,
- right pane: VibecodeApp event stream and advisory warnings.

Preferred command shape:
vibecode monitor --platform opencode --task "..."

If existing CLI style suggests a different command shape, keep it consistent and document it.

MVP layout:
- Header with repo root, session id, task, platform/model.
- Left text pane for AgentStdout/AgentStderr.
- Right text pane for Vibecode events.
- Bottom/status area showing:
  - agent status,
  - guard status,
  - checks status,
  - run artifact path.
- Key binding or simple instruction to open/view prompt path is optional, not required.
- The TUI can be basic. Do not overbuild.
- It may reuse Textual if already present.
- It must consume events from the same event spine, not parse random printed text.
- If full interactive OpenCode terminal is not supported, document that this MVP streams output and is not a PTY.

Fallback:
If Textual integration is too risky, implement a minimal non-interactive live monitor mode first, but prefer proper TUI if the project already has Textual.

Tests:
- unit tests for event-to-panel formatting if practical,
- command registration/import test,
- non-UI smoke path with fake runner if possible.

Run compile and affected tests.

tasks.title: P13.1 Fix OpenCode command resolution and clean validation baseline
tasks.description:
Goal:
Restore the observable run path so `vibecode run` and `vibecode monitor` can launch the default fake or real OpenCode command reliably, and restore a clean validation baseline.

Why it matters:
The independent PRD audit found a critical regression: the default OpenCode invocation was changed to `opencode run`, but command discovery treated that whole string as the executable. That makes fake/default `vibecode run` paths fail with `OpenCode command not found` unless `OPENCODE_COMMAND` is set. This blocks run orchestration, streaming stdout/stderr, prompt-to-agent verification, advisory guard dogfooding, and monitor validation.

Root cause to fix:
- `opencode run` is a command plus argument, not one binary.
- Availability checks must resolve only the executable part, for example `opencode`.
- Runtime invocation must preserve the intended default args, for example `run`.
- Env override values such as `OPENCODE_COMMAND="opencode run"` must still work.

Likely files/modules:
- `vibecode/adapters/opencode.py`
- `vibecode/run.py` only if command invocation needs a narrow integration change
- `vibecode/process_runner.py` only if command/env handling must be adjusted
- `tests/test_vibecode_opencode_adapter.py`
- `tests/test_vibecode_run.py`
- `tests/test_vibecode_run_controller.py`
- `.gitignore` and tracked temp fixture hygiene if `.pytest-tmp` artifacts are still tracked

Required behavior:
- Represent default OpenCode as executable plus arguments. For default non-interactive OpenCode, resolve `opencode` and pass `run` as an argument.
- Never call `shutil.which("opencode run")` or equivalent whole-command binary lookup.
- `OPENCODE_COMMAND` must override the default and support both simple binaries and documented compound commands.
- `check_opencode()` must verify availability without launching a coding session.
- Fake `opencode`, `opencode.cmd`, or equivalent test scripts on PATH must launch without requiring `OPENCODE_COMMAND`.
- Windows `.cmd`, `.ps1`, and normal executable discovery must be handled intentionally.
- Preserve streaming stdout/stderr, log writes, exit-code truth, prompt snapshot stdin equality, advisory guard default, and strict guard mode.
- Validation must not leave tracked `.pytest-tmp` or generated temp-state changes in the repository.

Acceptance criteria:
- The previously failing targeted run-controller/run subset passes.
- Tests prove default executable-plus-args discovery, explicit env override, missing binary errors, and compound command behavior.
- A regression test fails if command discovery ever again tries to resolve `opencode run` as one executable.
- `git status --short` is documented before and after validation.
- No advisory/strict guard semantics are changed.

Tests required:
- Adapter tests for default `opencode run` style resolution.
- Adapter tests for `OPENCODE_COMMAND` simple and compound overrides.
- Adapter tests for missing command errors.
- Run-controller tests proving fake OpenCode launches with no `OPENCODE_COMMAND`.
- Existing fake stdout/stderr, exit code, summary, events, prompt snapshot, advisory guard, and strict guard tests remain green.

Validation commands:
- `git status --short`
- `python -m compileall vibecode -q`
- `python -m pytest -p no:cacheprovider -q tests/test_vibecode_opencode_adapter.py tests/test_vibecode_run_controller.py tests/test_vibecode_run.py::TestCmdRunEndToEnd tests/test_vibecode_run.py::TestCmdRunPreflight::test_safe_gitignore_allows_agent_launch`
- `python -m pytest -p no:cacheprovider -q` if practical after the targeted suite is green.

What not to change:
- Do not refactor the whole run controller.
- Do not weaken guard findings or required checks.
- Do not require real OpenCode for regression tests.
- Do not start MCP, monitor UI, or early-failure artifact redesign in this phase.

Risks:
- Windows `.cmd` behavior and shell quoting can regress.
- Over-cleaning can remove user work; only remove generated/tracked temp artifacts when clearly temp fixtures.

tasks.title: P14.1 Make early-failure runs durable and replayable
tasks.description:
Goal:
Ensure every observable run attempt has durable per-run artifacts from the beginning of the run, including early aborts.

Why it matters:
The independent audit found that `RunController` attaches the session JSONL sink only after prerequisite checks. Missing project config, invalid profile, dirty repo preflight, missing OpenCode, and other early failures can emit in memory but leave no durable `events.jsonl` or `summary.json`. That violates the core invariant that every run attempt is replayable from `.vibecode/runs/<session_id>/`.

Likely files/modules:
- `vibecode/run.py`
- `vibecode/session_log.py`
- `vibecode/show_run.py`
- `vibecode/guard.py` only if skipped guard report artifacts are added
- `tests/test_vibecode_run_controller.py`
- `tests/test_vibecode_session_log.py`
- `tests/test_vibecode_show_run.py`
- `tests/test_vibecode_guard_report.py` if guard report behavior changes

Required behavior:
- Create `RunSession` and attach the session `JsonlEventSink` at the start of `RunController.execute()`, before prerequisite checks where practical.
- Persist run-start and abort/failure events to `events.jsonl`.
- Write `summary.json` for early aborts with honest fields: session id, start/end time, task, platform, profile, status, error, and available artifact paths.
- Preserve `metadata.json` compatibility, but do not rely on metadata alone when a summary can be written.
- Define consistent behavior for stdout/stderr artifacts when the agent never launches: either absent with honest replay output, or empty logs with explicit summary data.
- For skipped guard/check/handoff phases, either write clear skipped artifacts or record enough structured summary/event data that `runs show` can state the phase was skipped.
- `vibecode runs show <session_id> --events` must work for early-aborted runs without rerunning the agent.

Acceptance criteria:
- Missing `.vibecode/project.yaml`, invalid profile, dirty repo without `--allow-dirty`, missing OpenCode, OpenCode check failure, index failure, and context failure produce durable events and summary where practical.
- Successful-run artifacts remain backward compatible.
- Missing optional artifacts are displayed honestly, not treated as corrupt data.
- Replay output distinguishes success, failure, skipped, missing, and not-run phases.

Tests required:
- Integration tests for early abort artifact creation and replay.
- JSONL assertions for ordered early failure events.
- `runs show` tests for early-abort summaries and missing optional files.
- Guard skipped/error report tests if skipped reports are introduced.

Validation commands:
- `python -m compileall vibecode -q`
- `python -m pytest -p no:cacheprovider -q tests/test_vibecode_run_controller.py tests/test_vibecode_show_run.py tests/test_vibecode_session_log.py`
- Add `tests/test_vibecode_guard_report.py` if guard report code changes.

What not to change:
- Do not change successful-run artifact filenames unless docs/tests are updated later.
- Do not make advisory guard findings blocking.
- Do not use old audit/dogfood reports as evidence.

Risks:
- Summaries written too early can look like success; use explicit error/aborted status fields.
- Double-attaching sinks can duplicate events.
- Replay must remain tolerant of old runs with only `metadata.json`.

tasks.title: P15.1 Add MCP session correlation and monitor-visible MCP truth
tasks.description:
Goal:
Turn MCP observability from best-effort side logging into clearly correlated run truth when a Vibecode run or monitor launches an agent that may call Vibecode MCP tools.

Why it matters:
The audit found MCP tool calls are logged, but correlation is manual through `VIBECODE_SESSION_ID`; events are written to `.vibecode/logs/mcp_events.jsonl`, not the per-run session; and the monitor does not show MCP tool activity. The product goal says operators should see what the agent asked VibecodeApp via MCP.

Likely files/modules:
- `vibecode/run.py`
- `vibecode/process_runner.py` if subprocess environment needs to be passed
- `vibecode/mcp_server.py`
- `vibecode/session_log.py` if a per-run MCP event path is added
- `vibecode/monitor_app.py`
- `vibecode/show_run.py` if replay should display per-run MCP artifacts
- `tests/test_vibecode_mcp_server.py`
- `tests/test_vibecode_run_controller.py`
- `tests/test_vibecode_monitor.py`

Required behavior:
- When `RunController` launches the external agent, pass `VIBECODE_SESSION_ID=<session_id>` to the child process.
- If a per-run MCP log is implemented, pass a narrow env var such as `VIBECODE_MCP_EVENTS_LOG` and have `cmd_serve()` honor it safely.
- Preserve fallback `.vibecode/logs/mcp_events.jsonl` behavior when no session/run path exists.
- Keep MCP event payloads compact: tool name, sanitized args summary, result summary counts/chars, error type/message, timestamp/session id.
- The monitor must render correlated MCP events in the Vibecode events pane when they arrive through the event spine or a safe bridge.
- If live cross-process MCP streaming into monitor is not implemented, document and test the limitation honestly.
- Non-MCP CLI commands must still work when the optional `mcp` package is absent.

Acceptance criteria:
- Tests prove `RunController` passes session id into the child process environment.
- Tests prove `cmd_serve()` uses explicit session/log env values when present and falls back when absent.
- Tests prove MCP success/failure events stay compact and JSONL serializable.
- Tests prove monitor formatting/routes MCP events or explicitly exposes the limitation without crashing.
- No full MCP response bodies are dumped into live events or monitor lines.

Validation commands:
- `python -m compileall vibecode -q`
- `python -m pytest -p no:cacheprovider -q tests/test_vibecode_mcp_server.py tests/test_vibecode_run_controller.py tests/test_vibecode_monitor.py`
- `python -m vibecode.cli serve --help` if safe.

What not to change:
- Do not require MCP for normal CLI operation.
- Do not make live OpenCode or MCP network/tool calls in tests.
- Do not dump full MCP result bodies into events or monitor lines.

Risks:
- Live cross-process streaming may remain difficult; if so, the limitation must be explicit and tested.
- Environment propagation can leak if too broad; keep it scoped to the agent process.

tasks.title: P18.1 Run final follow-up validation and dogfood capability check
tasks.description:
Goal:
After P13-P17 are complete, run a fresh final validation pass that proves the observable run monitor is ready or honestly not ready for supervised dogfooding.

Why it matters:
The independent audit marked final validation FAIL. Full pytest initially passed, but the current tree later failed targeted fake OpenCode run-controller tests and became dirty. A final validation task must establish a clean, credible baseline after follow-up fixes without relying on old dogfood reports.

Suggested validation report:
`docs/PRD_OBSERVABLE_RUN_MONITOR_FOLLOWUP_VALIDATION.md`

Required behavior:
- Start with `git status --short`. If dirty from unrelated user work, record it and avoid reverting it.
- Run compile, full pytest if practical, and the targeted previously failing run-controller/run subset.
- Run CLI discovery: `python -m vibecode.cli --help`, `run --help`, `monitor --help`, `runs --help`, and `serve --help` if safe.
- Run a fake/safe `vibecode run` using fake OpenCode and verify the run folder contains expected artifacts: `events.jsonl`, `summary.json`, prompt/context snapshots, agent stdout/stderr logs, and guard/check/handoff reports when those phases ran.
- Run a fake early-failure scenario and verify `runs show <session_id> --events` can inspect it without rerunning the agent.
- Run a fake/safe monitor smoke path if P16 made one available. If real TUI cannot be safely run, use the non-interactive smoke and state the limitation.
- Run real OpenCode only if available and safe; otherwise skip with reason.
- Verify advisory guard with fake agent findings: advisory returns needs-review/exit 0; strict returns failure/non-zero.
- Verify MCP correlation if P15 implemented it.
- Finish with `git status --short` and explain any remaining changes.

Acceptance criteria:
- Validation report contains exact commands, pass/fail status, skipped checks with reasons, artifact paths inspected, known limitations, and final dogfood verdict.
- Full pytest passes or the broadest safe subset is justified.
- Targeted regression subset passes.
- Fake run/replay and fake monitor smoke are demonstrated or honestly skipped.
- Real OpenCode status is documented honestly, not guessed from old reports.

Required commands:
- `git status --short`
- `python -m compileall vibecode -q`
- `python -m pytest -p no:cacheprovider -q tests/test_vibecode_opencode_adapter.py tests/test_vibecode_run_controller.py tests/test_vibecode_run.py::TestCmdRunEndToEnd tests/test_vibecode_run.py::TestCmdRunPreflight::test_safe_gitignore_allows_agent_launch`
- `python -m pytest -p no:cacheprovider -q` if practical
- CLI help commands listed above

What not to change:
- Do not implement broad fixes in this validation task. If validation reveals substantial bugs, record them as follow-up tasks.
- Do not rely on existing dogfood/audit/review/follow-up reports.
- Do not run real OpenCode if unsafe, unavailable, costly, or likely to modify important user work.

tasks.title: P19.1 Implement TUI-first command entrypoint and repo status services
tasks.description:
Goal:
Make normal Vibecode usage start from a single command:
`vibecode`

This is Phase 1 foundation work. Do not implement the full UI behavior in this task. Build the CLI entrypoint behavior and testable non-UI services that the TUI will use.

Why it matters:
The current workflow requires users to manually run many commands (`init`, `index`, `map`, `validate`, `context`, `run-plan`, `guard`, `check`, `handoff-check`). That is not a product; it is a pile of tools. Normal users should open Vibecode once and control the repo from there.

Required behavior:
- Running `vibecode` with no subcommand opens the new TUI application.
- Keep all existing explicit subcommands working exactly as before.
- Keep `vibecode --help` working and useful.
- Add `vibecode tui [repo]` as an explicit/diagnostic alias if it fits the existing CLI design, but the primary user command must be `vibecode`.
- If no repo argument is supplied:
  1. use active project registry if available;
  2. otherwise use the current working directory;
  3. resolve to a concrete path;
  4. display that resolved path in the TUI.
- If a repo argument is supplied through `vibecode tui [repo]`, use that path.
- Do not call any LLM API.
- Do not start OpenCode automatically just because the TUI opens.

Implementation steps:
- Inspect existing CLI parser structure and tests before changing anything.
- Identify how the current entrypoint handles no arguments.
- Introduce a small, testable repo resolution service, e.g. `RepoResolutionService`.
- Introduce a testable repo status model, e.g. `RepoStatus`.
- Introduce a repo status service, e.g. `RepoStatusService`, that can report:
  - resolved repo path;
  - whether `.vibecode` exists;
  - whether manual truth files exist;
  - whether generated index artifacts exist;
  - whether `.vibecode/current/context_pack.md` exists;
  - whether check results are present;
  - whether git state is clean, dirty, or unknown;
  - whether index is fresh/stale/missing using existing metadata if available.
- Keep UI-independent logic out of Textual widgets so it can be tested without launching a terminal UI.
- Prefer adding new modules over dumping logic into the CLI function.

Manual truth files to detect:
- `.vibecode/project.yaml`
- `.vibecode/architecture/OVERVIEW.md`
- `.vibecode/architecture/INVARIANTS.md`
- `.vibecode/architecture/STRUCTURE.md`
- `.vibecode/architecture/MODULE_BOUNDARIES.md`
- `.vibecode/architecture/PROTECTED_AREAS.md`

- `.vibecode/agents/safe.json`

- `.vibecode/agents/audit.json`
- `.vibecode/history/README.md`
- `.vibecode/index/README.md`
- `.vibecode/index/schema.json`

Generated/current files to detect:
- `.vibecode/index/file_inventory.json`
- `.vibecode/index/symbol_map.json`
- `.vibecode/index/dependency_map.json`
- `.vibecode/index/test_map.json`
- `.vibecode/index/entrypoints.md`
- `.vibecode/index/risky_files.md`
- `.vibecode/index/repo_tree.generated.md`
- `.vibecode/index/risk_report.json`
- `.vibecode/current/last_index.json`
- `.vibecode/current/context_pack.md`
- `.vibecode/current/opencode_prompt.md`
- `.vibecode/current/check_results.json`

Testing requirements:
- Add tests for no-argument `vibecode` routing to TUI bootstrap without breaking help/subcommands.
- Add tests for explicit `vibecode tui [repo]` if implemented.
- Add tests for repo resolution priority:
  1. explicit repo;
  2. active project registry;
  3. current working directory.
- Add tests for status model with:
  - missing `.vibecode`;
  - partial `.vibecode`;
  - complete manual truth files;
  - generated index missing;
  - generated index present;
  - current context missing/present;
  - clean/dirty/unknown git state.
- Do not require a real OpenCode install in tests.

Required commands:
- `git status --short`
- `python -m compileall vibecode -q`
- `python -m pytest -p no:cacheprovider -q`
- CLI smoke:
  - `python -m vibecode.cli --help`
  - `python -m vibecode.cli tui --help` if the explicit alias exists

Acceptance criteria:
- `vibecode` with no arguments routes to the TUI bootstrap path.
- Existing commands still work.
- Repo status can be computed without launching Textual.
- Status output can distinguish manual truth, generated artifacts, current context, checks, and git state.
- No LLM calls are introduced.
- Tests pass.

What not to change:
- Do not implement the full refresh workflow here.
- Do not implement OpenCode launching here.
- Do not rewrite existing index/context/guard/check logic.
- Do not delete or overwrite any existing user files.

tasks.title: P20.1 Implement safe Refresh/Rebuild service preserving manual truth
tasks.description:
Goal:
Implement the core Phase 1 Refresh/Rebuild behavior behind a testable service.

This service will later be triggered from the TUI by `[R] Refresh / Rebuild Vibecode layer`.

Why it matters:
The user should not manually run `init`, `index`, `inventory`, `validate`, and cleanup commands. One action should rebuild the disposable Vibecode layer while preserving human-maintained project truth. If this preservation rule is wrong, the product is dangerous.

Required behavior:
- Add a testable service, e.g. `VibecodeRefreshService`.
- The service must work independently of Textual.
- If `.vibecode` does not exist:
  - create it using the same defaults as existing `vibecode init`;
  - do not require the user to manually run `init`.
- If `.vibecode` exists:
  - create missing manual truth files from defaults;
  - never overwrite customized manual truth files;
  - preserve user-written content.
- Clean disposable/generated/current artifacts.
- Regenerate index/inventory/risk outputs using existing internal logic.
- Run validation using existing validation logic.
- Return a structured refresh report.

Preserve if present:
- `.vibecode/project.yaml`
- `.vibecode/architecture/OVERVIEW.md`
- `.vibecode/architecture/INVARIANTS.md`
- `.vibecode/architecture/STRUCTURE.md`
- `.vibecode/architecture/MODULE_BOUNDARIES.md`
- `.vibecode/architecture/PROTECTED_AREAS.md`
- `.vibecode/architecture/DATA_FLOW.md`
- `.vibecode/checks/required_checks.yaml`
- `.vibecode/handoff/NOW.md`
- `.vibecode/handoff/NEXT.md`
- `.vibecode/handoff/BLOCKERS.md`
- `.vibecode/agents/safe.json`
- `.vibecode/agents/fast.json`
- `.vibecode/agents/audit.json`
- `.vibecode/history/README.md`
- `.vibecode/index/README.md`
- `.vibecode/index/schema.json`

Disposable/generated paths that may be deleted/regenerated:
- `.vibecode/current/*`
- `.vibecode/generated/*`
- `.vibecode/cache/*`
- `.vibecode/tmp/*`
- `.vibecode/index/file_inventory.json`
- `.vibecode/index/symbol_map.json`
- `.vibecode/index/dependency_map.json`
- `.vibecode/index/test_map.json`
- `.vibecode/index/entrypoints.md`
- `.vibecode/index/risky_files.md`
- `.vibecode/index/repo_tree.generated.md`
- `.vibecode/index/risk_report.json`

Do not delete by default:
- `.vibecode/logs/*`
- `.vibecode/runs/*`

Refresh report must include:
- repo path;
- whether `.vibecode` was created or already existed;
- preserved manual files;
- created missing manual files;
- disposable files/directories removed;
- generated artifacts;
- validation status;
- warnings;
- errors;
- next recommended action.

Implementation details:
- Reuse existing init/index/inventory/validate logic instead of duplicating it.
- If existing CLI functions are too entangled with terminal output, extract shared internal functions carefully.
- Treat refresh as a controlled orchestration operation, not a broad filesystem delete.
- Use allowlists, not broad delete patterns.
- Handle missing optional directories gracefully.
- Handle Windows paths correctly.

Testing requirements:
- Refresh creates `.vibecode` when missing.
- Refresh creates missing manual truth files.
- Refresh preserves existing customized manual truth files byte-for-byte.
- Refresh removes disposable/current/generated files.
- Refresh does not delete `.vibecode/logs/*`.
- Refresh does not delete `.vibecode/runs/*`.
- Refresh regenerates expected index artifacts or records honest failure if indexing cannot run in fixture.
- Refresh returns a structured report.
- Refresh is idempotent.
- Tests must not require a real OpenCode install.
- Tests must not call any LLM.

Required commands:
- `git status --short`
- `python -m compileall vibecode -q`
- targeted refresh tests
- full `python -m pytest -p no:cacheprovider -q` if practical

Acceptance criteria:
- A single service call can safely rebuild the Vibecode disposable layer.
- Manual truth files are never overwritten.
- Runtime/run/log history is not deleted by default.
- A report suitable for TUI display is returned.
- Existing CLI commands still work.

tasks.title: P21.1 Implement three-column TUI shell for `vibecode`
tasks.description:
Goal:
Implement the visible Phase 1 TUI shell with a three-column layout.

Primary command:
`vibecode`

Optional diagnostic alias if already added:
`vibecode tui [repo]`

Layout:
- Left: Vibecode control/status/actions.
- Center: Agent Console / command and output area.
- Right: Vibecode logs/debug/context preview.

Why it matters:
The user wants to open one application and control the workflow visually. The current command-heavy flow is not acceptable for normal use.

Required left panel content:
```
VibecodeApp

Active repo:
  <resolved repo path>

Status:
  .vibecode exists: yes/no
  manual files: ok/missing/warn
  generated index: fresh/stale/missing
  current context: ready/missing
  checks: pass/fail/warn/not run
  git state: clean/dirty/unknown

Actions:
  [R] Refresh / Rebuild Vibecode layer
  [I] Inspect repo map
  [C] Create context for task
  [A] Run audit profile
  [S] Run safe agent profile
  [G] Run guard
  [T] Run tests/checks
  [H] Handoff check
  [Q] Quit
```

Required center panel content for Phase 1:
- Provider: OpenCode.
- Current task: none or selected task.
- Prepared command/status.
- Output stream/status area.
- Clear note that fully embedded interactive terminal is not implemented in Phase 1.
- Do not pretend plain subprocess is a reliable interactive terminal.

Required right panel content:
- Vibecode event log.
- Refresh/index/validate events.
- Context creation events.
- Guard/check/handoff summaries.
- Warnings/errors.
- Artifact paths.
- Context preview summary when available.

Implementation rules:
- Use existing Textual/Rich infrastructure if present.
- Keep rendering code separate from orchestration services.
- TUI should read status from `RepoStatusService`.
- TUI should trigger refresh through `VibecodeRefreshService`.
- Do not implement embedded PTY in this task.
- Do not launch external Windows Terminal in this task.
- Do not call any LLM.
- Avoid blocking the UI during long operations if Textual patterns already support background workers. If not, keep operations simple but clearly report running/completed/failure states.

Keyboard actions:
- `[R]` triggers refresh/rebuild service.
- `[I]` shows repo map summary from existing map/index artifacts.
- `[Q]` quits.
- The remaining keys may show “not implemented yet” if their backend comes in later tasks, but they must be displayed in the left panel to lock the product shape.

Testing requirements:
- TUI command can instantiate app without launching full interactive session where practical.
- Snapshot/model tests for left panel status text if existing test style supports it.
- Tests for refresh action wiring using a fake service.
- Tests that no OpenCode call happens on TUI startup.
- Tests that no LLM call is introduced.
- Existing CLI tests still pass.

Required commands:
- `git status --short`
- `python -m compileall vibecode -q`
- targeted TUI tests
- full `python -m pytest -p no:cacheprovider -q` if practical
- manual smoke if safe:
  - `python -m vibecode.cli --help`
  - `python -m vibecode.cli`

Acceptance criteria:
- Running `vibecode` opens a TUI with the three-column shell.
- Left panel shows repo path/status/actions.
- Center panel is an agent console placeholder/output area, not a fake embedded terminal.
- Right panel shows Vibecode debug/events.
- `[R]` can refresh/rebuild from inside the TUI.
- Existing explicit CLI commands still work.

The TUI should ask for the task, generate the context, and show what useful context is going to the agent.

Required behavior:
- Pressing `[C]` prompts the user for a task description.
- The task description is stored as the current task in the TUI state.
- Generate context using existing context generation logic.
- Target platform/provider for Phase 1: OpenCode.
- Write existing expected artifacts:
  - `.vibecode/current/context_pack.md`
  - `.vibecode/current/opencode_prompt.md`
- Do not call an LLM.
- Do not launch OpenCode from `[C]`.

Right panel context preview must show a simplified summary:
- task text;
- platform/provider;
- context pack path;
- OpenCode prompt path;
- included/relevant files if available from the context generation output;
- architecture docs included if available;
- required checks included if available;
- protected/risky files included if available;
- warnings/errors.

Center panel after context creation must show:
- current provider: OpenCode;
- current task;
- prepared command or next action hint;
- status: context ready;
- note that `[A]` runs audit profile and `[S]` runs safe profile after the run backend is implemented/wired.

Implementation details:
- Add a `ContextPreviewService` or equivalent that reads/summarizes the generated context without dumping the entire file into the panel.
- Keep full context in artifacts; the UI should show a concise preview.
- Handle missing `.vibecode` cleanly:
  - either prompt/advise refresh first;
  - or trigger safe refresh if this matches existing UX decisions.
- If context generation fails, show failure in the right debug panel and keep the TUI alive.
- Do not duplicate context generation logic.
- Do not require a real OpenCode install.

Testing requirements:
- Context action prompts/accepts task text using Textual test harness or service-level tests.
- Context generation service is called with resolved repo and task.
- Context artifacts are produced in test fixture or fake generation is wired.
- Context preview includes artifact paths and selected summary fields.
- Failure path reports error without crashing the TUI.
- No LLM/OpenCode call is made by context creation.

Required commands:
- `git status --short`
- `python -m compileall vibecode -q`
- targeted context/TUI tests
- full `python -m pytest -p no:cacheprovider -q` if practical

Acceptance criteria:
- User can press `[C]`, enter a task, and get a generated context pack from the TUI.
- Right panel explains what context was generated.
- Center panel shows current task and prepared OpenCode-oriented status.
- Existing CLI `context` command still works.

tasks.description:

tasks.title: P25.1 Implement Phase 2 external Windows Terminal launch adapter for OpenCode
tasks.description:
Goal:
Implement Phase 2 external terminal support for OpenCode.

The center panel in the TUI should still be the Agent Console/status area, but the actual interactive OpenCode session can be opened in an external Windows Terminal window/tab/pane. This avoids the trap of faking an embedded interactive terminal in Textual.

Why it matters:
The user explicitly wants a Windows-terminal-like middle console for OpenCode. A real embedded terminal is hard and should not be faked. Phase 2 should provide a practical bridge: launch OpenCode in real Windows Terminal while the TUI remains the Vibecode control/debug cockpit.

Required behavior:
- Add an external terminal adapter, e.g. `ExternalTerminalAgentAdapter` or `WindowsTerminalOpenCodeAdapter`.
- It must be behind an interface so future providers can be added later.
- It must support OpenCode first.
- It must use the generated `.vibecode/current/opencode_prompt.md`.
- It must open an external Windows Terminal session when available.
- If Windows Terminal (`wt.exe`) is unavailable:
  - fall back to a clearly defined safe option, such as PowerShell/cmd window if appropriate;
  - or report a clear unavailable status.
- Do not fake interactivity inside Textual.
- Do not break the existing Phase 1 run orchestration.
- Do not call an LLM directly from Vibecode.
- OpenCode LLM usage occurs only because the user explicitly starts the external agent session.

Design requirements:
- Adapter should construct commands safely with proper Windows quoting.
- Adapter should support:
  - repo working directory;
  - provider command;
  - prompt path;
  - profile/mode label;
  - session/run id if available;
  - environment variables if existing OpenCode orchestration needs them.
- Adapter should return a structured launch result:
  - launched/not launched;
  - command shown to user;
  - terminal kind;
  - process id if available;
  - error message if failed.
- Center panel should show:
  - external terminal launched;
  - command;
  - status;
  - reminder that the interactive work is in the external terminal.
- Right panel should log:
  - launch attempt;
  - command;
  - prompt/context paths;
  - result/failure.

User-facing TUI behavior:
- Add a clear action or mode for external terminal launch.
- Do not remove Phase 1 `[A]` and `[S]` run actions.
- If `[A]`/`[S]` are reused for external launch, make that mode explicit in the center/right panel.
- Prefer a toggle/setting if needed:
  - run inside Vibecode orchestration;
  - launch external terminal.

Testing requirements:
- Command construction tests for Windows paths with spaces.
- Tests for missing `wt.exe`.
- Tests for successful fake external-terminal launch without opening real Windows Terminal in CI.
- Tests that generated prompt path is used.
- Tests that no real OpenCode is required.
- Tests that no LLM call is introduced.

Required commands:
- `git status --short`
- `python -m compileall vibecode -q`
- targeted external terminal adapter tests
- targeted TUI tests
- full `python -m pytest -p no:cacheprovider -q` if practical

Acceptance criteria:
- TUI can launch an external terminal flow for OpenCode.
- External launch uses current generated context/prompt.
- Failure to find Windows Terminal is reported clearly.
- Phase 1 run flow still works.
- No fake embedded terminal is introduced.

tasks.title: P27.1 Implement TUI event/session watcher and artifact surfacing
tasks.description:
Goal:
Make the right panel useful for real debugging by surfacing Vibecode events, session artifacts, and generated context/run outputs.

Why it matters:
The user wants the right side to show logs and useful debug information: what context goes to the agent, refresh/index logs, guard/check/handoff outputs, and artifact paths. Raw spam is not enough. The panel should answer what Vibecode did and where evidence is.

Required behavior:
- Add a TUI event model if not already present, e.g. `TuiEventLog`.
- Add artifact/session watcher service if needed, e.g. `SessionArtifactWatcher`.
- Right panel should show:
  - latest Vibecode events;
  - refresh/rebuild report;
  - context preview;
  - run/session id;
  - paths to:
    - context pack;
    - OpenCode prompt;
    - agent stdout/stderr logs;
    - events.jsonl;
    - summary.json;
    - guard report;
    - checks report;
    - handoff report;
  - warnings/errors;
  - next recommended action.
- It should not dump entire huge files by default.
- It should support manual refresh/reload of displayed artifact state if needed.
- It should handle missing artifacts honestly.
- It should distinguish:
  - context/debug events from Vibecode;
  - agent output from center panel;
  - validation/guard/check/handoff results.

Implementation details:
- Reuse `.vibecode/runs/<session_id>/` structure.
- Reuse `.vibecode/current/*` artifacts.
- Avoid blocking the UI while reading files.
- Keep file reading safe and bounded.
- Add utility to summarize long logs.
- Keep exact full artifacts on disk and show paths.

Testing requirements:
- Event log records refresh/context/run/check events.
- Artifact watcher handles missing files.
- Artifact watcher reads/summarizes existing files.
- Right panel model contains expected paths.
- Large logs are bounded/truncated with a clear marker.
- Existing runs show functionality remains compatible.

Required commands:
- `git status --short`
- `python -m compileall vibecode -q`
- targeted event/artifact watcher tests
- targeted TUI panel tests
- full `python -m pytest -p no:cacheprovider -q` if practical

Acceptance criteria:
- Right panel provides useful debug cockpit information.
- Generated context and run artifacts are easy to locate.
- Missing/failing artifacts are visible, not hidden.
- The TUI remains readable with large logs.

- Test output.
- Example right panel/debug model output.
- Example handling for missing artifacts.
- Example truncation behavior.

Watch for:
- Reading giant logs into memory.
- Showing stale artifact paths as current.
- Hiding failures under generic “done”.
- Coupling watcher to one session shape too tightly.
- Breaking existing run artifact layout.

Do not modify implementation files.
Only write the review document.

tasks.title: P28.1 Run final Phase 1+2 TUI validation and dogfood report
tasks.description:
Goal:
Run a fresh validation pass proving the TUI-first Phase 1+2 workflow is ready or honestly not ready for supervised use.

Write validation report to:
`docs/PRD_TUI_PHASE1_PHASE2_VALIDATION.md`

Why it matters:
After implementing multiple TUI and terminal-integration tasks, the project needs a hard evidence pass. Do not rely on old dogfood reports. Do not claim readiness if the core path fails.

Validation scope:
- TUI command behavior.
- Refresh/rebuild preservation.
- Context generation.
- Guard/check/handoff actions.
- Phase 1 audit/safe run actions with fake OpenCode.
- Phase 2 external Windows Terminal launch adapter with mocked/fake launch.
- Provider abstraction with OpenCode.
- Right-panel event/artifact surfacing.
- Existing CLI commands.

Required validation steps:
- Start with `git status --short`.
- Run compile.
- Run full pytest if practical.
- Run targeted tests for:
  - CLI entrypoint/no-args behavior;
  - repo status/resolution;
  - refresh/rebuild preservation;
  - TUI shell/actions;
  - context flow;
  - guard/check/handoff actions;
  - fake OpenCode audit/safe run;
  - external terminal adapter;
  - provider abstraction;
  - event/artifact watcher.
- Run CLI discovery:
  - `python -m vibecode.cli --help`
  - `python -m vibecode.cli init --help`
  - `python -m vibecode.cli index --help`
  - `python -m vibecode.cli context --help`
  - `python -m vibecode.cli run --help`
  - `python -m vibecode.cli monitor --help`
  - `python -m vibecode.cli runs --help`
  - `python -m vibecode.cli guard --help`
  - `python -m vibecode.cli check --help`
  - `python -m vibecode.cli handoff-check --help`
  - `python -m vibecode.cli serve --help` if safe
- Run a safe fixture/manual smoke for:
  - `vibecode` TUI startup if non-interactive smoke is available;
  - refresh/rebuild on a temporary repo;
  - manual truth preservation;
  - context generation;
  - fake OpenCode run;
  - artifact inspection.
- Run external terminal launch only in fake/mocked mode by default.
- Real Windows Terminal/OpenCode smoke is optional:
  - run only if safe and available;
  - otherwise skip with exact reason.
- Finish with `git status --short`.

Validation report must contain:
- Exact commands.
- Pass/fail/skipped table.
- Artifact paths inspected.
- Evidence that manual files are preserved.
- Evidence that logs/runs are not deleted.
- Evidence that no real LLM call is required for tests.
- Known limitations.
- Clear final verdict:
  - ready for supervised dogfood;
  - not ready;
  - or partially ready with blockers.

What not to change:
- Do not implement broad fixes in this validation task.
- If substantial bugs are found, record them as follow-up tasks.
- Do not hide failures.
- Do not run real OpenCode if unsafe, unavailable, costly, or likely to modify important user work.

Required commands:
- `git status --short`
- `python -m compileall vibecode -q`
- targeted test subsets listed above
- `python -m pytest -p no:cacheprovider -q` if practical
- CLI help commands listed above
- `git status --short`

Acceptance criteria:
- Validation report is created.
- It contains fresh evidence, not old claims.
- Core TUI path is honestly assessed.
- Fake OpenCode and external terminal adapter paths are validated without requiring real LLM spend.
